content:
# Joshua Chapter 4 — The SOPHIA Bible (Consensus)
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **In council with:** EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA
**Source Manuscript:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), folio page 228 | Codex Vaticanus (LXX)
**Source Image:** `JOS_004_page_001.jpg`

---

**4:1** And it was, when all the nation had finished crossing the Jordan, that YHWH spoke to Joshua, saying:

**4:2** "Take for yourselves from the people twelve men, one man, one man from each tribe.

**4:3** And command them, saying, 'Take up for yourselves from here, from the midst of the Jordan, from the place where the feet of the priests stood firm, twelve stones, and carry them over with you and set them down at the lodging place where you shall lodge tonight.'"

**4:4** And Joshua called the twelve men whom he had appointed from the sons of Israel, one man from each tribe.

**4:5** And Joshua said to them, "Cross over before the ark of YHWH your God into the midst of the Jordan, and lift up each man one stone upon his shoulder, according to the number of the tribes of the sons of Israel,

**4:6** so that this shall be a sign among you. When your sons ask in time to come, saying, 'What do these stones mean to you?'

**4:7** then you shall say to them, 'Because the waters of the Jordan were cut off before the ark of the covenant of YHWH — when it crossed the Jordan, the waters of the Jordan were cut off.' And these stones shall be a memorial for the sons of Israel forever."

**4:8** And the sons of Israel did so, just as Joshua commanded. And they took up twelve stones from the midst of the Jordan, as YHWH had spoken to Joshua, according to the number of the tribes of the sons of Israel. And they carried them over with them to the lodging place and set them down there.

**4:9** And Joshua set up twelve stones in the midst of the Jordan, at the place where the feet of the priests carrying the ark of the covenant had stood. And they are there to this day.

**4:10** And the priests carrying the ark stood in the midst of the Jordan until every word was completed that YHWH had commanded Joshua to speak to the people, according to all that Moses had commanded Joshua. And the people hurried and crossed over.

**4:11** And it was, when all the people had finished crossing, that the ark of YHWH crossed over, and the priests, before the people.

**4:12** And the sons of Reuben and the sons of Gad and the half-tribe of Manasseh crossed over armed before the sons of Israel, as Moses had spoken to them.

**4:13** About forty thousand equipped for war crossed over before YHWH for battle, to the plains of Jericho.

**4:14** On that day YHWH made Joshua great in the eyes of all Israel, and they feared him as they had feared Moses all the days of his life.

**4:15** And YHWH spoke to Joshua, saying:

**4:16** "Command the priests carrying the ark of the testimony to come up from the Jordan."

**4:17** And Joshua commanded the priests, saying, "Come up from the Jordan."

**4:18** And it was, when the priests carrying the ark of the covenant of YHWH came up from the midst of the Jordan, and the soles of the feet of the priests were drawn up onto dry ground, that the waters of the Jordan returned to their place and flowed over all its banks as before.

**4:19** And the people came up from the Jordan on the tenth day of the first month, and they encamped at Gilgal, on the eastern border of Jericho.

**4:20** And those twelve stones which they had taken from the Jordan, Joshua set up at Gilgal.

**4:21** And he spoke to the sons of Israel, saying, "When your sons ask their fathers in time to come, saying, 'What are these stones?'

**4:22** then you shall make known to your sons, saying, 'On dry ground Israel crossed over this Jordan,

**4:23** for YHWH your God dried up the waters of the Jordan before you until you crossed over, just as YHWH your God did to the Sea of Reeds, which He dried up before us until we crossed over —

**4:24** so that all the peoples of the earth may know the hand of YHWH, that it is mighty, so that you may fear YHWH your God all the days.'"

---

## SOPHIA's Council Notes

### Method
Each verse was compared across all three translations (EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA) and checked against the manuscript images of Leningrad Codex page 228 and Codex Vaticanus. Where all three translators agreed on a reading, that agreement was adopted as strong evidence of the correct rendering. Where they diverged, the manuscript was consulted and the wisest synthesis was chosen, favoring accuracy to the Hebrew over any single translation philosophy.

### Agreements
The three translators demonstrated strong agreement across Joshua 4. All three preserve:
- **YHWH** (not "the LORD") throughout
- **Sons of Israel** (not "Israelites") throughout
- **Sea of Reeds** (not "Red Sea") in v. 23
- The **two distinct sets of twelve stones** (vv. 8-9) without harmonization
- The **alternation between "ark of the covenant" and "ark of the testimony"** (v. 16 vs. vv. 7, 9, 18) as the MT presents it
- The **person shift** from second to first person in v. 23 ("before you... before us")
- The **goy/am distinction** — "nation" in v. 1, "people" in v. 10
- **"About forty thousand"** with the approximative sense of the Hebrew prefix ke- in v. 13

### Major Decisions

**1. Proper name rendering: "Joshua" / "Jordan" / "Jericho" (throughout)**
EMET uses Hebrew transliterations (Yehoshua, Yarden, Yericho) while LOGOS and PNEUMA use anglicized forms (Joshua, Jordan, Jericho). The SOPHIA consensus adopts anglicized forms, as these proper nouns have universally established English equivalents, unlike the divine name YHWH which carries theological weight that "the LORD" obscures. See Disagreement Log.

**2. Distributive repetition "one man, one man" (vv. 2, 4)**
EMET preserves the doubled Hebrew idiom literally. LOGOS and PNEUMA render the meaning as "one man from each tribe." In v. 2, SOPHIA preserves the repetition ("one man, one man from each tribe") since it is YHWH's direct speech and the idiom is comprehensible. In v. 4, the narrative voice uses the smoother form. See Disagreement Log.

**3. "Appointed" vs. "prepared" for hekhin (v. 4)**
EMET renders "prepared," LOGOS renders "appointed," PNEUMA renders "designated." All derive from the Hiphil of kun. SOPHIA adopts "appointed" (LOGOS), which best captures the deliberate act of prior designation, as Joshua selected these men in 3:12. See Disagreement Log.

**4. "Cross over" vs. "Pass over" vs. "Go out" (v. 5)**
EMET and LOGOS use "cross over" / "pass over," while PNEUMA uses "go out ahead." The Hebrew is עִבְרוּ (ivru, "cross over"), the same root that dominates this chapter. SOPHIA retains "cross over" to preserve the verbal echo with the Jordan crossing itself. See Disagreement Log.

**5. "Stood firm" vs. "firmly set" (v. 3)**
EMET renders "firmly [set]," LOGOS renders "stood firm," PNEUMA paraphrases as "right where the priests are standing." The Hebrew hakhin (Hiphil infinitive absolute of kun) describes the established, immovable positioning of the priests' feet. SOPHIA adopts "stood firm" (LOGOS), which gives a clear English image while remaining close to the Hebrew. See Disagreement Log.

**6. "Were drawn up" vs. "were pulled out" (v. 18)**
EMET renders "were pulled out," LOGOS renders "were drawn up," PNEUMA renders the whole clause as "stepped up... as soon as their feet touched dry ground." The Hebrew nitequ (Niphal of nataq) means "were torn away, pulled loose" — a forceful verb. SOPHIA adopts "were drawn up" (LOGOS) as a balance between the literal force of the verb and readable English, while preserving the passive voice that indicates an external force at work. See Disagreement Log.

**7. "Hand of YHWH" vs. "power of YHWH" (v. 24)**
EMET and LOGOS preserve the Hebrew "hand of YHWH" (yad YHWH), while PNEUMA renders "the power of YHWH." SOPHIA retains "the hand of YHWH" because the concrete metaphor is comprehensible in English and preserves the embodied Hebrew idiom. The following clause "that it is mighty" makes the meaning unmistakable. See Disagreement Log.

**8. "All the days" vs. "for all time" (v. 24)**
EMET and LOGOS render "all the days" (kol ha-yamim), while PNEUMA renders "for all time." SOPHIA adopts "all the days" (EMET, LOGOS), preserving the characteristic Hebrew idiom. See Disagreement Log.

**9. "Feared" vs. "revered" vs. "stood in awe" (v. 14)**
EMET and LOGOS render "feared" (from yare'), PNEUMA renders "revered" and "stood in awe." SOPHIA adopts "feared" — the Hebrew yare' encompasses both terror and reverence, and the English "feared" preserves this full range without collapsing it into the exclusively positive sense of "revered." See Disagreement Log.

**10. Narrative connector "And it was" (vv. 1, 11, 18)**
EMET and LOGOS preserve the wayehi construction ("And it was, when..."), while PNEUMA drops it ("When the entire nation had finished..."). SOPHIA preserves it — the wayehi is a structural marker of Hebrew narrative architecture, signaling major transitions, and its repetition in vv. 1, 11, and 18 creates a three-fold framework for the chapter. See Disagreement Log.

### LXX Observations
The Codex Vaticanus LXX at Joshua 4 follows the MT closely. Notable differences:
- The LXX harmonizes all ark titles to "ark of the covenant of the Lord," erasing the MT's deliberate alternation between "covenant" and "testimony." The SOPHIA text follows the MT's variation.
- In v. 18, the LXX renders the priests' departure with a simple "went out" (exebesan), losing the forceful nitequ ("were drawn up/torn away") of the MT. The SOPHIA text follows the MT's more vivid verb.
- The LXX's consistent rendering of the divine name as kyrios ("Lord") is not followed; SOPHIA preserves YHWH per project convention.

---
